titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the City of Brooklyn, Department of Health vital statistics ARC.160
titlestmt.author: Matthew Gorham
titlestmt.sponsor: This collection was originally processed and described as part of the project, "Uncovering the Secrets of Brooklyn's 19th Century Past: Creation to Consolidation," funded by the Council on Library and Information Resources, with additional support from The Gladys Krieble Delmas Foundation.

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2026-04-24 20:49:08 +0000.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: English, Latin script.

did.unittitle: City of Brooklyn, Department of Health vital statistics

origination.label: Creator
origination.corpname: Brooklyn (New York, N.Y.). Department of Health

did.abstract: Bound volumes of weekly vital statistics reports compiled and published by the Brooklyn Department of Health for each year covered in the date span.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Some if not all of these reports were deposited with the Long Island Historical Society by the Department of Health, presumably as they were produced.

bioghist.head: Historical note
bioghist.p: The amended charter of the City of Brooklyn, written in 1873, mandated the formation of a department of health. The head of this department was called the Health Commissioner. The Health Commissioner's duties included the removal and burial of the dead, operation and maintenance of ambulance services, the registration of births, marriages and deaths, the registration and maintenance of vital statistics, and the regulation of sanitary standards in city buildings. In times of emergency, such as the outbreak of a dangerous disease in the city, extraordianry powers could be conferred upon the Health Commissioner to address the crisis.

list.head: Source:
list.item: Brooklyn Daily Eagle Almanac (Brooklyn: Brooklyn Daily Eagle, 1892), 35-36.

phystech.head: Physical Characteristics and Technical Requirements
phystech.p: If digital surrogates exist, they should be used in place of the originals whenever possible.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Identification of item, date (if known); City of Brooklyn, Department of Health vital statistics collection, ARC.160, Box and Folder number; Brooklyn Public Library, Center for Brooklyn History.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Originally minimally processed to the collection level, the collection was rehoused and the finding aid revised by Dee Bowers in April 2026.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The City of Brooklyn, Department of Health vital statistics collection dates from 1873 to 1897. The collection consists of bound volumes of weekly vital statistics reports compiled and published by the Department of Health for each year covered in the date span. The reports list the number of deaths for each week, the causes of those deaths, the number of deaths in each voting ward, and general demographic information about the deceased (including gender, ethnicity/nationality, age, etc). The reports do not list the names of the deceased.
Additionally, the collection includes a report on deaths in the Brooklyn Theatre Fire from December 5 1876.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The materials in this collection are in the public domain. While many items at the Center for Brooklyn History are unrestricted, we do not own reproduction rights to all materials. Be aware of the several kinds of rights that might apply: copyright, licensing and trademarks. The researcher assumes all responsibility for copyright questions.
